ItacPersonItem.Notes: A letter of his from Padua, addressed to Camillo Vizzani in Rome, dated 22 July 1566, is in the Ranuzzi MSS Ph 12670 at the Harry Ransom Center. The letter mentions Scipione Gonzaga and two Academies, presumably the Eterei Academy in Padua, and the Oziosi academy in Bologna, to which all three apparently belonged: `... non restero` di dargli di V. S. quella notizia che io debbo, et pigliero` fra l`altre occasione dell`Accademia nostra, essendone egli protettore...`. Varoli wishes that when Gonzaga will be in Bologna `... gli fosse fatto dalla nostra Academia una sessione pubblica nella quale si dicessero le sue lode et se gli facesse dono della Academia...` In particular, Varoli asks Vezzani whether he would be willing to use for such occasion a canzone he had previously composed.
ItacPersonItem.Citation: Lorenza Gianfrancesco and Simone Testa, directed by Jane Everson, Denis Reidy, Lisa Sampson, assisted by Thomas Denman, for the Italian Academies Database project.
